jobs.title: Lead DevOps Engineer, Foundry RnD Job MasterCard
jobs.description: Mastercard powers economies and empowers people across 200+ countries and territories. Together with our customers, we build a sustainable, inclusive economy by enabling secure, simple, smart, and accessible digital payments. Our technology, innovation, partnerships, and networks deliver products and services that help people, businesses, and governments reach their full potential.
responsibilities: Drive Platform Infrastructure: Own DevOps and infrastructure for MLOps and agentic AI systems, establishing reusable patterns for CI/CD, scalable inference, orchestration, observability, and cost control. Design secure, scalable, repeatable systems using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to support R&D workloads.

Build secure CI/CD & automation systems: Enable secure tool access, workload isolation, and infrastructure for LLM-backed APIs and MCP servers, while partnering with security and compliance on access control, infrastructure governance and auditability.
Ensure Reliability & Observability: Implement monitoring, logging, and alerting. Tune observability for ML-specific workloads to ensure performance, reliability, and operational insight.
Provide Technical Leadership: Offer hands-on leadership across DevOps and platform initiatives. Review code, enforce best practices, improve tooling, and promote clean, well-tested infrastructure.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with ML, software, and platform engineers to design deployment strategies, scope work, manage agile deliverables, and meet milestones.
requirements: Extensive DevOps Experience: 8–12+ years in DevOps, SRE, or platform engineering, including senior/lead roles. Experience designing end-to-end infrastructure systems, solving scale/performance challenges, and operating platforms in production.

Cloud & Infrastructure Expertise: Strong skills in cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and AI/ML components such as Databricks, Azure ML, and MLflow. Deep experience with Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and orchestration tools like Terragrunt.

Container & Orchestration Mastery: Expertise in Kubernetes and Docker, including how they optimise ML development workflows. Experience with container security, networking, and cluster management at scale.

AI/ML Platform Knowledge: Understanding of ML workflow requirements—model registries, feature stores, AI agents, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques, and frameworks like LangChain/LlamaIndex.
Leadership & Mentorship: Ability to translate ambiguous goals into clear plans, guide engineers, and lead technical execution.
jobs.howToApply: Click here to apply

jobs.title: IAM Enterprise Architect (AI) Job Standard Chartered Bank
responsibilities: We are seeking an accomplished and highly delivery driven and strategic thinking IAM Enterprise Architect with experience in securing AI identities to join our Threats, Architecture and Controls within the Identity & Access Management organization.

The IAM Enterprise Architect will be responsible for defining the strategic vision, architectural patterns, and control frameworks for managing identities in an AI-drive ecosystem. This includes securing the “Human-to-AI” interface, managing the identities of autonomous AI agents, and ensuring theat AI model training and inference processes adhere to Zero Trust Principles.

The IAM Enterprise Architect will ensure the tactical and strategic plans and solutions are continuously strengthen to respond to the increasing threats and changing sophistication in attack tactics while keeping up with the growth and changes of business.

The IAM Enterprise Architect will bridge the gap between business priorities, cybersecurity policies, and technical delivery teams to deliver next-generation cyber defences against identity threats to protect our Customers.

The successful candidate will provide strong technical security direction for identity access controls and capabilities for securing identities in Enterprise AI Solution, and lead and partner with engineers, architects, compliance teams, and business stakeholders to deliver against the capabilities roadmap, enhance user experience, and align with regulatory and cybersecurity standards. The role demands a strong awareness of the cyber threat landscape and understanding of cyber threat actors and their attack tactics, identity access controls, and AI technologies and capabilities. This individual will drive delivery health, ensure achievement of OKRs, and shape the future of IAM solutions, reporting directly to the Head of IAM Threats Architecture and Controls.
requirements: 15+ years of total experience as IT professional with at least 5 years in working on AI solutions and 5 years in designing and implementing Identity Access Management Solutions.
In-depth knowledge of identity protocols such as Oauth2, OIDC, SAML, specifically how they are extended to support non-human identity such as Agent Identity.
In-depth knowledge of management of Agentic Identity such as how to assign, verify, and revoke permissions for autonomous agents.
Deep expertise in Zero Trust Architecture and hands on experience in implementing these for AI solutions.
Implementation experience in designs using Attribute-Based Access Control and Policy as Code.
jobs.howToApply: Click here to apply

Contribute to the evolution toward hybrid and cloud architectures in a pragmatic, value‑driven manner
requirements: Strong .NET development background

Solid backend architecture and system design skills
Experience with Git‑based version control

Experience with cloud-based data storage solutions (AWS Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Azure Synapse Analytics).
Knowledge of data standards such as FHIR and HL7, and experience working with electronic medical records (EMRs) or health management systems.
